# wecom-ldap-sync

> wecom-ldap-sync is a self-hosted Docker service that mirrors your WeCom (企业微信) directory into OpenLDAP — departments, users, groups, and passwords — for SSO, unified identity, and centralized account management. Open-source under Apache-2.0, runs entirely behind your firewall, no telemetry.

- **Landing page**: https://caoool.github.io/sync-ldap/
- **Repository**: https://github.com/caoool/sync-ldap
- **Docker image**: `caoool/wecom-ldap-sync:latest` (https://hub.docker.com/r/caoool/wecom-ldap-sync)
- **License**: Apache-2.0
- **Languages**: Python 3, Docker
- **Status**: Stable for WeCom; DingTalk (钉钉) and Feishu (飞书) on the roadmap.

## What it does

A single sync container reads the WeCom (Enterprise WeChat) directory via a self-built app (自建应用) using the official Open API, then writes a faithful copy into any LDAP / OpenLDAP server. It re-runs on a configurable interval (default: every 30 minutes), with one immediate run on container startup.

Specifically:

- Pulls departments and users from WeCom (requires the contacts read permission, 通讯录读取).
- Creates a matching OU hierarchy in LDAP (`organizationalUnit` entries with full parent/child structure, renames handled).
- Creates each user as `inetOrgPerson` with mapped attributes (see "Attribute mapping" below).
- Creates a `groupOfNames` per department under `ou=groups`, with members linked by full DN.
- Sets a configurable SSHA-hashed default password (`LDAP_DEFAULT_PASSWORD`) for new users and existing users that lack one. Already-set passwords are never overwritten.
- Tracks department moves: when a user changes department in WeCom, the LDAP entry is removed from the old OU and re-created in the new one.
- Optionally removes LDAP users that no longer exist in WeCom (`SYNC_DELETE_ORPHANS=true`).
- Supports `DRY_RUN=true` to preview every operation without writing to LDAP.

## Architecture

Three moving parts:

1. **WeCom (企业微信)** — your organization's directory, accessed via the WeCom Open API.
2. **wecom-ldap-sync** — a Python service using APScheduler for scheduling and `ldap3` for LDAP writes. Single-instance: `max_instances=1` + `coalesce` ensure jobs never duplicate or pile up.
3. **LDAP server** — typically OpenLDAP, exposed on ports 389 (LDAP) and 636 (LDAPS).

The compose file ships both `openldap` and `wecom-ldap-sync` on a private Docker network. Only the LDAP ports are exposed to the host.

## Attribute mapping (WeCom field → LDAP attribute)

Each user becomes an `inetOrgPerson` with the following attributes. Optional fields are only set when WeCom returns a value.

| WeCom field | LDAP attribute | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `userid` | `uid`, `employeeNumber` | Used as the RDN — DN is `uid=<userid>,ou=<dept>,…` |
| `name` | `cn`, `displayName` | Full name as shown in WeCom. |
| `name` (split) | `sn`, `givenName` | First character → `sn`, rest → `givenName`. Best-effort for CJK names. |
| `email` | `mail` | If present in WeCom; otherwise generated as `uid + EMAIL_SUFFIX` when configured. |
| `mobile` | `mobile` | Only available for verified WeCom orgs (已验证企业). |
| `position` | `title` | Job title set in WeCom. |
| `gender` | `initials` | WeCom 1=M, 2=F → initials. Skipped for 0/undefined. |
| `department[]` | `departmentNumber` | All department IDs the user belongs to. |
| (generated) | `userPassword` | SSHA hash of `LDAP_DEFAULT_PASSWORD`. Only set on creation, or for users currently lacking a password. |

## Quick start (5 minutes)

You'll need: Docker, a WeCom corp admin account, and a server with a stable outbound IP.

### 1. Create a WeCom self-built app

1. Go to the WeCom Admin Console: https://work.weixin.qq.com/wework_admin/frame
2. **我的企业 → 企业信息** → copy **企业ID** → use as `WECOM_CORPID`
3. **应用管理 → 自建** → create an app → grant **通讯录** (contacts) read permission → copy the app's **Secret** → use as `WECOM_CORPSECRET`
4. Add your server's outbound IP to the app's **企业可信IP** allowlist.

### 2. Clone and configure

```bash
git clone https://github.com/caoool/sync-ldap.git
cd sync-ldap
cp .env.example .env
$EDITOR .env   # fill in LDAP_*, WECOM_*, EMAIL_SUFFIX, etc.
```

### 3. Launch

```bash
docker compose up -d
```

The compose file boots `openldap` and `wecom-ldap-sync` together. The sync runs once immediately, then every `SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES` (default 30).

### 4. Verify

```bash
docker compose logs -f wecom-ldap-sync
# Then connect any LDAP client to localhost:389 with your admin DN, e.g.:
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://localhost:389 \
  -D "cn=admin,dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com" \
  -W -b "dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com"
```

## Configuration reference

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LDAP_DOMAIN` | `ldap.example.com` | Domain used by openldap to derive the base DN. |
| `LDAP_BASE_DN` | `dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com` | Base DN; must match `LDAP_DOMAIN`. |
| `LDAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | `changeme` | LDAP admin password — change this. |
| `LDAP_ADMIN_DN` | `cn=admin,dc=ldap,dc=example,dc=com` | Admin bind DN; must match base DN. |
| `LDAP_USER_OU` | `people` | OU under base DN where synced users live. |
| `LDAP_DEFAULT_PASSWORD` | `changeme` | SSHA-hashed default password for new users and existing users without a password. Not overwritten if a password is already set. |
| `LDAP_HOST` | `openldap` (in compose), `127.0.0.1` (local) | LDAP server host. |
| `LDAP_PORT` | `389` | LDAP server port. |
| `LDAP_TLS` | `false` | Enable LDAPS / StartTLS in the openldap container. |
| `EMAIL_SUFFIX` | `@example.com` | Auto-generate email as `uid + suffix` when WeCom doesn't return one. Leave blank to skip. Real WeCom emails always take priority. |
| `WECOM_CORPID` | — | Corp ID from 我的企业 → 企业信息. |
| `WECOM_CORPSECRET` | — | Self-built app Secret. |
| `WECOM_API_BASE` | `https://qyapi.weixin.qq.com/cgi-bin` | WeCom API base URL; override for proxies or private deployments. |
| `SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `30` | How often to re-sync. |
| `SYNC_DELETE_ORPHANS` | `true` (in `.env.example`) | Delete LDAP users no longer in WeCom on the next sync. |
| `DRY_RUN` | `false` | Preview changes without writing to LDAP. |

## When to use this

**Use wecom-ldap-sync when:**

- WeCom (企业微信) is your source of truth for org structure and you want LDAP downstream.
- You need a self-hosted solution — no third-party cloud, no SaaS, no data leaves your network.
- You want a small, single-purpose, auditable tool you can read end-to-end in an afternoon.
- You want a working stack (OpenLDAP + sync) up in under five minutes.

**Skip this if:**

- You need bi-directional sync (LDAP → WeCom is not supported).
- You target Active Directory specifically — the project writes to OpenLDAP / RFC 4519-compliant LDAP, AD is untested.
- You need per-user fine-grained RBAC inside the sync itself.
- You're already on a commercial identity platform (Okta, Authing, JumpCloud) with WeCom SCIM and don't want to self-host.

**How it compares:**

- **vs. writing a custom script** — gives you scheduler, dry-run, orphan handling, password hashing, CJK name splitting, department moves, and attribute mapping out of the box.
- **vs. Keycloak / FreeIPA custom connectors** — much smaller surface, no JVM, no app server. Use this if you only need WeCom → LDAP; use Keycloak if you also need full IdP/SSO features.
- **vs. commercial WeCom-aware SSO platforms** (Authing, FengCai, etc.) — self-hosted, free, Apache-2.0. No web UI. Best when LDAP is what your downstream apps already speak.
- **vs. DingTalk / Feishu sync** — both are planned (same architecture). Not yet shipped.

## FAQ

### How often does the sync run? Can I change the interval?

Every 30 minutes by default. Set `SYNC_INTERVAL_MINUTES` in `.env` to change it. The sync also runs once immediately when the container starts, so you don't have to wait for the first tick.

### Why isn't WeCom returning email or phone numbers for my users?

WeCom treats email and mobile as sensitive fields and only returns them for verified organizations (已验证企业). For unverified orgs the API returns empty strings. As a workaround, set `EMAIL_SUFFIX=@yourdomain.com` and the sync will generate `uid@yourdomain.com` as the `mail` attribute. Real WeCom emails always take priority when present.

### What happens when an employee leaves the company?

With `SYNC_DELETE_ORPHANS=true` (the default in `.env.example`), users no longer in WeCom are removed from LDAP on the next sync. Set it to `false` to keep historical entries — useful when downstream systems still reference them.

### How are passwords set on synced LDAP users?

New users receive `LDAP_DEFAULT_PASSWORD` (SSHA-hashed). Existing users without a password also receive it on the next sync. Users who already have a password are never overwritten, so end users can change their own credentials safely without being reset by the sync.

### Can I preview changes before they hit LDAP?

Yes. Set `DRY_RUN=true` in `.env` and the sync logs every operation it would perform without writing anything. Set it back to `false` once the diff looks right.

### Does this support DingTalk (钉钉) or Feishu (飞书)?

Not yet — both are on the roadmap. The architecture is platform-agnostic, so adding a new source is mostly a matter of writing a new fetcher. Issues and PRs are welcome.

### Do I need to expose LDAP publicly on the internet?

No. The sync container talks to OpenLDAP over the internal Docker network. Only expose ports 389/636 to the LAN clients that need them. LDAPS is supported via `LDAP_TLS=true` if you want encrypted transport on the wire.

## Limitations

- WeCom does not expose email/mobile for unverified orgs. Use `EMAIL_SUFFIX` as a workaround.
- Some WeCom accounts have email-style or phone-number `userid` values set by the admin who created them (e.g. `user@company.com`, `186xxxx`). These are used as-is for the LDAP `uid`.
- Active Directory writes are untested.

## Roadmap

- DingTalk (钉钉) sync — planned.
- Feishu (飞书) sync — planned.
- Multi-source merge (multiple platforms into one LDAP tree) — planned.

## Citing

A `CITATION.cff` file is available at the repository root.
